pr.title: Match the initial GObj callback functions in some of the vi files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched the first small callback functions in several melee/vi translation units by replacing leading assembly stubs with simple C wrappers. Most matched functions take an HSD_GObj*, load gobj->hsd_obj, cast it to HSD_JObj*, and call HSD_JObjAnimAll. One vi0601 callback simply forwards to un_8031CAAC. The PR added new vi source/header files and inserted their C object files before the remaining asm objects in obj_files.mk so only the newly matched functions moved out of assembly.
what_changed: Removed the matched leading assembly implementation of un_8031D6E4 from asm/melee/vi/vi0402.s and added src/melee/vi/vi0402.c with a C implementation calling HSD_JObjAnimAll((HSD_JObj*) gobj->hsd_obj).
Removed un_8031E6CC and un_8031E6EC from asm/melee/vi/vi0601.s and added C implementations: un_8031E6CC calls un_8031CAAC, while un_8031E6EC animates the HSD_JObj stored in the GObj.
Removed un_8031F990 and un_8031F9B4 from asm/melee/vi/vi1201v1.s and added equivalent C callbacks in src/melee/vi/vi1201v1.c.
Removed un_803204C0 and un_803204E4 from asm/melee/vi/vi1201v2.s and added equivalent C callbacks in src/melee/vi/vi1201v2.c.
Removed un_8032110C, un_80321130, and un_80321154 from asm/melee/vi/vi1202.s and added equivalent C callbacks in src/melee/vi/vi1202.c.
Added per-file headers declaring the newly matched functions and including <sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h> for HSD_GObj.
Added src/melee/vi/vi.h with a declaration for un_8031CAAC(void), used by vi0601.c.
Updated obj_files.mk to compile the new C objects before the corresponding remaining asm objects for vi0402, vi0601, vi1201v1, vi1201v2, and vi1202.
smart_moves: Targeted very small, repeated callback patterns first: load the HSD object from offset 0x28 of the GObj and call HSD_JObjAnimAll. This converted multiple functions with low semantic risk.
Kept the remaining unmatched functions in their existing asm files, avoiding a larger rewrite of each vi translation unit.
Placed each new C object immediately before the corresponding asm object in obj_files.mk, preserving section/order expectations while peeling off only the matched leading symbols.
Used the existing baselib types HSD_GObj and HSD_JObj rather than raw offset arithmetic, making the callback intent explicit while still matching the observed assembly.
Added local headers for each vi file so future decompilation work can include stable prototypes for these callbacks.
decomp_lessons: A GObj callback whose assembly is just lwz r3, 0x28(r3) followed by HSD_JObjAnimAll can be expressed as HSD_JObjAnimAll((HSD_JObj*) gobj->hsd_obj).
When a file begins with a few matched functions followed by unmatched assembly, add a C object before the asm object rather than moving the whole file at once.
Tiny wrapper functions are useful decompilation footholds in large vi files: they reduce asm while introducing correct headers and object ordering for future matches.
If one callback is only a direct call-through to another existing symbol, a void wrapper such as un_8031E6CC calling un_8031CAAC can replace the prologue/call/epilogue assembly.
Preserve unknown address-based symbol names when no better semantic names are evidenced; this PR did not rename un_8031D6E4-style symbols.
naming_conventions: New vi headers use lowercase include guards matching the file name pattern, such as _vi0402_h_, _vi0601_h_, _vi1201v1_h_, _vi1201v2_h_, and _vi1202_h_.
Unknown functions remain in the un_<address> style, for example un_8031D6E4, un_8031E6EC, un_8031F990, un_803204C0, and un_8032110C.
Per-translation-unit vi source/header filenames mirror the existing assembly filenames: vi0402, vi0601, vi1201v1, vi1201v2, and vi1202.
Function declarations in the new headers omit parameter names, using forms such as void un_8032110C(HSD_GObj*);.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched the repeated PowerPC pattern: function prologue, lwz r3, 0x28(r3), branch-and-link to HSD_JObjAnimAll, function epilogue.
Mapped the lwz from offset 0x28 of the incoming GObj to the typed field access gobj->hsd_obj.
Used an explicit cast from HSD_GObj object payload to HSD_JObj* before calling HSD_JObjAnimAll.
Matched a simple forwarding wrapper in vi0601 where the body only calls un_8031CAAC and returns.
Deleted only the matched symbols from the asm files, leaving the next global symbols such as un_8031D708, un_8031E710, un_8031F9D8, un_80320508, and un_80321178 in assembly.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining vi GObj callbacks that load gobj->hsd_obj and call HSD_JObjAnimAll.
Inspect remaining assembly symbols after un_8031D708, un_8031E710, un_8031F9D8, un_80320508, and un_80321178 for similar wrapper patterns.
Search for other direct wrapper functions that only call un_8031CAAC or related vi helpers.
Look for opportunities to replace address-based un_ names with semantic names once surrounding vi behavior is understood.
